name: Carmona and Vía Verde de los Alcores Loop
name_original: El Viso del Alcor a Carmona - circular
description: This moderate 20.4-mile mountain bike loop combines historical sites and natural landscapes along the Vía Verde de los Alcores near Carmona.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Viso Del Alcor are situated in the La Campiña region of Seville, characterized by its distinctive landscapes. The area features the Alcores ridge, an inclined plateau within the Guadalquivir Depression, offering varied terrain. The landscape includes fertile plains, river valleys, and agricultural areas with olive groves and orange orchards. This geological setting provides a mix of accessible paths and more challenging trails for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around El Viso Del Alcor The most popular mountain bike trail is Carmona and Vía Verde de los Alcores Loop, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route follows the Vía Verde de los Alcores, offering a relatively flat path through picturesque countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Los Alcores Greenway – Hacienda de Gandul loop from El Viso del Alcor, a moderate 22.4 miles (36.0 km) path. This route explores the Los Alcores Greenway, winding through the region's characteristic agricultural and natural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Los Alcores Ridge loop from El Viso del Alcor, a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail leading through the Alcores ridge, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around El Viso Del Alcor is defined by the Alcores ridge, the Vía Verde de los Alcores, and expansive agricultural plains. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in El Viso Del Alcor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Viso Del Alcor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Viso Del Alcor?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around El Viso Del Alcor, offering a variety of experiences. The komoot community has rated these routes with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Viso Del Alcor?
A: Yes, there are easy mountain bike trails available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32080574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seville loop from El Viso del Alcor&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 25 km route that can be completed in under 2 hours. This route offers a gentle introduction to the region's terrain.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in El Viso Del Alcor?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails in El Viso Del Alcor are rated as moderate, with 11 out of 13 routes falling into this category. This means they often involve varied terrain, some elevation changes, and require a reasonable level of fitness. There are no difficult trails listed in this guide.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see while mountain biking in El Viso Del Alcor?
A: Mountain biking in El Viso Del Alcor offers scenic views from the Alcores ridge, which is often described as a 'balcony in La Campiña'. You can enjoy panoramic vistas from spots like the Balcony of Los Alcores and the Calvario Viewpoint. The trails also wind through picturesque countryside characterized by stone pines, Mediterranean scrubland, olive groves, and orange orchards.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around El Viso Del Alcor is rich in history. You can find attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Gate of Seville&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carmona Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2778669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hacienda de Gandul&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical site you might encounter on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14604266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Alcores Greenway – Hacienda de Gandul loop from El Viso del Alcor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in El Viso Del Alcor?
A: The region is best enjoyed in dry conditions. Riding after rain is not recommended due to the sandy soil, which can cause mud to stick to bikes. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and drier weather, making them ideal for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in El Viso Del Alcor are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40359463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Alcores Ridge loop from El Viso del Alcor&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.4 km trail that explores the prominent Alcores ridge.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, many natural trails in Spain, including greenways, are generally dog-friendly if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or specific trail rules before heading out with your pet.

Q: What is the Vía Verde de los Alcores, and are there mountain bike routes along it?
A: The Vía Verde de los Alcores is a 25-kilometer natural trail that follows the old 'Tren de los Panaderos' railway line. It offers a relatively flat and accessible path with sandy soil, suitable for various cycling levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992075858/carmona-and-via-verde-de-los-alcores-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carmona and Vía Verde de los Alcores Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular mountain bike route that utilizes this greenway.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Los Alcores Greenway?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40352892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Alcores Greenway – Las Encinas Singletrack loop from El Viso del Alcor&lt;/a&gt; is a 30.2 km moderate route that takes you along sections of the Los Alcores Greenway, offering a chance to experience the region's characteristic landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in El Viso Del Alcor?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 60 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the picturesque countryside with its olive groves and orange orchards, and the accessible greenways like the Vía Verde de los Alcores.
tips: Carmona has several viewpoints, but the most well-known is the Mirador de la Asomada del Ribero, located at the entrance to the town, and also the Mirador del Parador, next to the Alcázar of King Pedro. Both offer spectacular views.

The Church of San Pedro is one of the most emblematic churches in Carmona (Seville), recognizable above all for its tower known as La Giraldilla, due to its resemblance to the Giralda of Seville.

Built in the 15th century on the site of the former hermitage of the Virgen de la Antigua, its Baroque tower, called La Giraldilla, was originally designed by Hernán Ruiz the Younger around 1565 and completed in 1784.

The Vía Verde de los Alcores is a 25-km nature trail that connects Carmona with Alcalá de Guadaíra, passing through Mairena del Alcor and El Viso del Alcor, following the old route of the legendary Tren de los Panaderos (Bakers' Train).

It's perfect for walking, cycling, horseback riding, and even wheelchair access in some sections. The terrain is mostly sandy soil, surrounded by countryside, stone pines, and Mediterranean scrubland.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Carmona and Vía Verde de los Alcores Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in the towns along the route, such as Carmona or El Viso del Alcor, which serve as common starting points for loops incorporating the Vía Verde de los Alcores. Look for public parking areas within these towns.

faqs.question: Is the Carmona and Vía Verde de los Alcores Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Vías Verdes are generally accessible for various outdoor activities, and dogs on a leash are usually permitted. However, always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations, especially when passing through populated areas or near agricultural land.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring, to avoid the intense heat of summer in Andalusia. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable riding conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: No, permits or entrance fees are generally not required to cycle on the Vía Verde de los Alcores or the connecting country roads that form this loop. Vías Verdes are public, traffic-free paths.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vía Verde de los Alcores Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. Sections of the Vía Verde feature asphalt, paved surfaces, and compacted gravel. However, the loop also includes unpaved sections, country roads, and areas with sand or a coating to prevent erosion. Be aware that some parts, particularly from Gandul to Carmona, may have potholes and gravel, and there are also cobblestone sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views of the Sevillian countryside, including lowlands ('Vega') running parallel to the Los Alcores hills. The route is often bordered by trees like stone pines, scrubland, and palm formations. From Carmona, which sits atop the Alcores range, there are commanding views of the vast and fertile plain known as La Vega del Corbones.

faqs.question: What are the notable historical features or landmarks on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a journey through history, passing near ancient villages and significant archaeological sites. You'll encounter the Church of San Pedro, Carmona, and ride along the Vía Verde de Los Alcores (Carmona), which follows the historic 'Tren de los Panaderos' (Baker's Train). You can also enjoy the Carmona Viewpoint for expansive vistas.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The overall difficulty is rated as moderate. While the Vía Verde sections are generally accessible, the full loop incorporates more challenging segments. Some unpaved or technical areas might require good fitness or advanced riding skills, and you might even need to push your bike in certain parts, especially if venturing into more technical areas around the hills.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the loop passes through or near towns like Carmona and El Viso del Alcor, you will find various options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. Carmona, in particular, offers a range of services for visitors.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily utilizes sections of the Vía Verde de los Alcores. It also intersects with other local paths such as Camino Viejo, Camino de la Alunada, La Pajarita, Carril del Judio, and Vereda de Paradas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is approximately 32.9 km (20.4 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours and 4 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.
